proposals.action: Append learned preference: '- 2026-07-12: in the Weekend digest, get elapsed-time framing right — verify any "a year after / months since / anniversary of" phrasing against the event's dated fact from the daily briefs (1× 👎 with reason "The war hasn't started a year ago…" on the 2026-07-11 Iran lead, which said "a year after their war" when the war opened 28 Feb 2026).'
proposals.evidence: st-dd9d52b17487 (2026-07-11-weekend Iran lead) 👎 with verbatim reason 'The war hasn't started a year ago…'; the war opened 28 Feb 2026 per the pipeline's own dated facts, so 'a year after their war' is a ~4.5-month error. Vote carries a written reason -> qualifies for bounded auto-apply.

proposals.action: Week in headlines: cite ONLY a URL actually fetched this run (curl/proxy, 200/3xx confirmed) — never reconstruct a plausible outlet slug from memory; reuse the daily brief's exact verified URL when the item came from a daily; drop a recap item rather than cite an unverified link.
proposals.evidence: 2026-07-11-weekend cited two Al Jazeera URLs that return HTTP 404 (are-the-us-and-iran-at-war-again; zaporizhzhias-mayor-...-outskirts), confirmed via curl GET and WebFetch; all seven daily-brief AJ links in the same window resolve 200. _posts/2026-07-12-evaluator.md Patch 1.

proposals.action: Week in headlines: when stating an elapsed interval ('a year after', 'months since', 'anniversary of'), compute it from the event's carried date and sanity-check; do not assert a duration not derived from a dated fact in this pipeline.
proposals.evidence: Same st-dd9d52b17487 downvote; the rm-2 (2026-07-05) date-anchor patch made the writer add temporal framing and it produced a wrong interval. _posts/2026-07-12-evaluator.md Patch 2.

proposals.action: arXiv preprints: if the paper's HTML author block does not render, query OpenAlex works API (api.openalex.org/works?search=<title>) and then Semantic Scholar BEFORE marking '(affiliation not listed)'; only mark unlisted when all three fail. Never guess.
proposals.evidence: 2026-07-11-weekend ran ~37% '(affiliation not listed)' (7 of ~19 papers) because HTML author blocks did not render, while 2026-07-10 AI/ML ran ~15%. The single-method chain marks unlisted instead of falling back. _posts/2026-07-12-evaluator.md Patch 3.
